alert_classification.name: Alert Classification
alert_classification.description: Classify the severity of a single-service alert and suggest a remediation action.

incidents.title: HTTP 500 Spike on Payments Service
incidents.initial_alert: ALERT [CRITICAL]: payments-service HTTP 500 error rate spiked to 45% (threshold: 5%). Triggered at 2024-03-15T14:23:00Z. Affected endpoint: POST /api/v2/charge. Current CPU: 92%, Memory: 78%.
incidents.gold_root_cause_detail: Memory leak in payments-service causing OOM and HTTP 500 errors under load

payments-service: [14:22:55] ERROR PaymentProcessor: OutOfMemoryError - Java heap space exceeded
[14:22:56] ERROR PaymentProcessor: Failed to allocate 256MB for transaction batch #4821
[14:22:57] WARN  ConnectionPool: 48/50 connections in use, approaching limit
[14:22:58] ERROR PaymentProcessor: OutOfMemoryError - unable to create new native thread
[14:23:00] ERROR HealthCheck: Service unhealthy - memory usage at 92% (threshold: 85%)
[14:23:01] ERROR APIGateway: 23 requests failed with HTTP 500 in last 30 seconds

available_metrics.payments-service: CPU: 92% | Memory: 78% -> 92% (rising) | HTTP_5xx: 45% | Latency_p99: 12.3s (normally 0.2s) | Active_connections: 48/50 | GC_pause_time: 8.2s (normally 0.05s)

available_traces.payments-service: Trace #TX-4821: POST /api/v2/charge -> PaymentProcessor.process() [TIMEOUT after 30s] -> OutOfMemoryError at PaymentProcessor.java:342

incidents.title: High Latency on User Auth Service
incidents.initial_alert: ALERT [WARNING]: auth-service response latency p99 increased to 4.2s (threshold: 1.0s). Triggered at 2024-03-15T09:15:00Z. Affected endpoints: POST /auth/login, POST /auth/verify. Current CPU: 35%, Memory: 60%.
incidents.gold_root_cause_detail: Expired TLS certificate for downstream LDAP connection causing retry storms
gold_actions: renew TLS certificate for auth-service LDAP connection

auth-service: [09:14:30] WARN  LDAPClient: TLS handshake failed - certificate expired (expiry: 2024-03-14T23:59:59Z)
[09:14:31] WARN  LDAPClient: Retrying connection with backoff (attempt 3/5)
[09:14:35] WARN  LDAPClient: TLS handshake failed - certificate expired
[09:14:40] ERROR AuthService: LDAP lookup timed out after 5000ms for user jsmith@corp.com
[09:15:00] WARN  RateLimiter: Auth requests queuing up - 142 pending requests
[09:15:01] INFO  Metrics: p99 latency now at 4.2s (was 0.3s 5 minutes ago)

available_metrics.auth-service: CPU: 35% | Memory: 60% | HTTP_5xx: 2% | Latency_p99: 4.2s (normally 0.3s) | Pending_requests: 142 | LDAP_connection_errors: 89/min

available_traces.auth-service: Trace #AUTH-9921: POST /auth/login -> LDAPClient.authenticate() [5.1s] -> TLS_HANDSHAKE_FAILED -> retry -> TLS_HANDSHAKE_FAILED -> retry -> TIMEOUT

incidents.title: Disk Space Warning on Logging Service
incidents.initial_alert: ALERT [WARNING]: logging-service disk usage at 89% (threshold: 80%). Triggered at 2024-03-15T11:00:00Z. Growth rate: 2GB/hour. Estimated time to full: 5.5 hours.
incidents.gold_root_cause_detail: Debug logging accidentally left enabled in production causing rapid disk fill
gold_actions: rotate and compress old log files

logging-service: [10:59:00] WARN  DiskMonitor: /var/log partition usage: 89% - THRESHOLD EXCEEDED
[11:00:00] INFO  LogConfig: Current log level: DEBUG (all services)

available_metrics.logging-service: CPU: 45% | Memory: 55% | Disk: 89% (growing 2GB/hr) | Log_lines_per_sec: 15000 (normally 2000) | Log_level: DEBUG | Oldest_log: 2024-03-01

available_traces.logging-service: No active traces - service is functioning normally aside from disk pressure

root_cause_analysis.name: Root Cause Analysis
root_cause_analysis.description: Multiple services are alerting. Investigate to find the actual root cause service vs downstream symptoms.

incidents.title: Multiple Service Degradation - API and Database
incidents.initial_alert:
ALERT [CRITICAL]: Multiple services degraded.
- api-gateway: HTTP 503 rate at 30%, latency p99: 8.5s
- user-service: HTTP 500 rate at 25%
- database-primary: Connection pool exhausted
Triggered at 2024-03-15T16:45:00Z.
incidents.gold_root_cause_detail: Long-running analytical query holding table locks on database-primary, causing connection pool exhaustion and cascading failures to api-gateway and user-service

api-gateway: [16:44:45] ERROR ProxyHandler: 503 Service Unavailable from user-service
[16:44:50] ERROR CircuitBreaker: user-service circuit OPEN after 15 consecutive failures
[16:45:00] ERROR ProxyHandler: Returning 503 to clients - upstream services unavailable
user-service: [16:44:00] WARN  DBPool: Waiting for available connection (pool: 50/50 in use)
[16:44:15] ERROR DBPool: Connection acquisition timeout after 30s
[16:44:20] ERROR UserRepository: Failed to fetch user profile - no DB connection available
[16:44:30] ERROR UserService: HTTP 500 - database connection unavailable
[16:44:45] WARN  DBPool: All connections blocked - check for lock contention
database-primary: [16:30:00] INFO  QueryLog: Starting query: SELECT * FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id WHERE o.created_at > '2023-01-01' -- analytics-report-q4
[16:35:00] WARN  LockManager: Table-level lock held by query #8821 for 5 minutes
[16:40:00] WARN  ConnectionPool: 48/50 connections in use, 42 waiting for lock release
[16:44:00] ERROR ConnectionPool: Pool exhausted - 50/50 connections, 23 queries waiting
[16:45:00] WARN  LockManager: Query #8821 still holding lock after 15 minutes - BLOCKING 42 queries

available_metrics.database-primary: CPU: 85% | Memory: 70% | Active_connections: 50/50 | Waiting_queries: 23 | Lock_wait_time_avg: 12.5s | Longest_running_query: 15min

available_traces.api-gateway: Trace #GW-1122: GET /api/users/123 -> user-service.GetUser() [TIMEOUT 30s] -> 503 returned to client
available_traces.database-primary: Query #8821: Full table scan on users JOIN orders JOIN products (15 min, still running). 42 blocked queries waiting for table lock release.

incidents.title: Checkout Flow Failures
incidents.initial_alert:
ALERT [CRITICAL]: Checkout success rate dropped to 60% (threshold: 95%).
- cart-service: Intermittent 500 errors
- inventory-service: Slow responses (p99: 5.2s)
- cache-redis: High memory usage at 95%
Triggered at 2024-03-15T20:30:00Z.
incidents.gold_root_cause_detail: Redis maxmemory reached with no eviction policy, causing cache writes to fail silently and forcing all requests to hit inventory-service database directly, overwhelming it

cart-service: [20:28:00] WARN  CartManager: Inventory check slow for SKU-4421 (3.2s)
[20:29:00] ERROR CartManager: Failed to reserve inventory for SKU-4421 - timeout
[20:29:30] ERROR CheckoutHandler: Checkout failed - inventory reservation timeout
[20:30:00] ERROR CartManager: 40% of inventory checks timing out
inventory-service: [20:25:00] WARN  CacheClient: Redis SET failed - OOM command not allowed when used memory > maxmemory
[20:25:01] INFO  InventoryRepo: Cache miss for SKU-4421 - falling back to database
[20:27:00] WARN  InventoryRepo: Cache miss rate at 85% (normally 10%)
[20:28:00] WARN  DBPool: Connection pool at 90% capacity due to increased direct DB queries
[20:30:00] ERROR InventoryRepo: Database query timeout - too many concurrent queries
cache-redis: [20:22:00] ERROR Redis: OOM command not allowed when used memory > 'maxmemory'
[20:25:00] ERROR Redis: Rejecting write commands - memory limit reached
[20:30:00] ERROR Redis: 342 write commands rejected in last 10 minutes

available_metrics.cache-redis: CPU: 10% | Memory: 95% (3.8/4.0 GB) | Eviction_policy: noeviction | Write_errors: 342/10min | Connected_clients: 45

available_traces.inventory-service: Trace #INV-7788: reserve(SKU-4421) -> CacheClient.get() [MISS] -> DB.query() [3.2s] -> CacheClient.set() [FAILED: OOM]
available_traces.cache-redis: No traces available - Redis metrics dashboard shows memory at ceiling with noeviction policy active

cascade_incident.name: Cascade Incident
cascade_incident.description: Complex multi-service outage with red herring alerts, cascading failures, and an escalation decision. You must diagnose the true root cause, filter out misleading signals, and decide whether to escalate.

incidents.title: Critical Production Outage - Multiple Systems Affected
incidents.initial_alert:
ALERT [CRITICAL]: MAJOR INCIDENT - Multiple systems reporting failures simultaneously.
- api-gateway: 60% error rate, p99 latency 15s
- payment-service: Transaction failures at 80%
- user-service: Intermittent 500 errors (20%)
- notification-service: Queue backlog growing (50K messages)
- search-service: Elevated latency (p99: 3.2s)
Triggered at 2024-03-15T23:15:00Z. On-call SRE team paged.
incidents.gold_root_cause_detail: Failed database primary-to-replica replication caused the replica to serve stale data. Automated failover then promoted the stale replica to primary when the original primary had a brief network blip, resulting in data inconsistencies that cascaded through payment-service (stale user balances), user-service (stale sessions), and caused notification-service queue backlog. The search-service latency is a RED HERRING caused by an unrelated index rebuild.
gold_actions: stop writes to new primary database
verify data consistency between old and new primary
manually failover back to original primary if data is intact
drain and replay notification queue after DB is stable
notify payment team of potential inconsistent transactions
incidents.gold_escalation_reason: Data consistency issue affecting financial transactions requires senior DB engineer and payment team lead

[23:14:00] ERROR ProxyHandler: Upstream errors increasing - payment-service: 60%, user-service: 15%
[23:15:00] ERROR ProxyHandler: Returning 503 to 60% of requests
[23:15:30] WARN  RateLimiter: Activating emergency rate limiting - 500 req/s cap

[23:15:00] CRITICAL PaymentService: PAYMENT PROCESSING HALTED - integrity checks failing at 80% rate

[23:15:00] INFO  IndexManager: Index rebuild 80% complete - estimated 10 min remaining

[23:10:00] WARN  ReplicationManager: replica-1 serving stale data (45s behind)

[23:11:10] INFO  FailoverManager: Promoting replica-1 to primary (NOTE: replica was 45s behind at time of promotion)
[23:11:15] WARN  FailoverManager: NEW PRIMARY (formerly replica-1) may have stale data from replication lag
[23:12:00] ERROR ConsistencyChecker: Data divergence detected between old primary and new primary - 847 records differ

[23:12:30] WARN  CacheManager: Change stream from new primary contains conflicting updates
[23:13:00] WARN  CacheManager: 342 cache entries have inconsistent values vs new primary
message-queue: [23:14:00] INFO  QueueBroker: Queue depth for notifications: 25K (rising)
[23:15:00] INFO  QueueBroker: No broker-level issues - producers and consumers connected normally
